_comment: Canonical wire envelope for all pub/sub messages crossing component boundaries. g8ee publishes G8eMessage (g8ee component), g8eo publishes ResultMessage (g8eo component). Both follow this shape. Consumers: g8ee (Python), g8eo (Go), g8ed (JS).
_comment.field_naming: FIELD NAMING: Wire format models use 'operator_id' instead of 'id' for clarity in cross-component contexts where the entity type may not be immediately obvious. This maps directly to OperatorDocument.core_identity.id (see operator_document.json _comment.field_naming for the full convention).

envelope._description: Outer envelope wrapping every pub/sub message on any channel.

source_component.description: Component that published this message. See status.json component.name.

event_type.description: Event type string. Must be a value from events.json.

operator_id.description: Operator identifier. Required for all g8eo-originated messages. Maps from OperatorDocument.core_identity.id.

operator_session_id.description: Operator session identifier. Required for all g8eo-originated messages.

web_session_id.description: Browser session identifier. Required when message is targeted to a specific frontend tab.

instance_id.description: Source component instance identifier. g8ee-only, optional.

system_fingerprint.description: SHA256 fingerprint of the operator host system. g8eo-only, included in g8eo-originated messages.

payload.description: Event-specific payload. Shape is defined by event_type. See result_payloads.json and command_payloads.json.

metadata.description: Auxiliary routing metadata (e.g. reply_to_topic). Not used by g8eo.

_comment.g8eo: g8eo's ResultMessage uses `payload` as a raw JSON blob (json.RawMessage). Fields `instance_id` and `metadata` are absent in g8eo-originated messages. Fields `system_fingerprint` and `source_component` are always present in g8eo messages.
_comment.g8ee: g8ee's G8eMessage uses `payload` as a typed dict. Fields `system_fingerprint` is absent in g8ee-originated messages.
_comment.channels: Messages are published to channels defined in channels.json. The envelope is the same regardless of channel.
